title: Nahum 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nahum
paragraphs: 1 This is the message against Nineveh, the book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite:
2 The LORD is a jealous God who takes revenge. The LORD takes revenge and is full of anger. The LORD takes revenge on his foes. He keeps anger for his enemies.
3 The LORD is slow to anger and great in power. The LORD will not leave the guilty unpunished. His path is in the windstorm and storm. The clouds are the dust under his feet.
4 He commands the sea and dries it up. He makes all the rivers run dry. Bashan and Carmel wither, and the flower of Lebanon fades.
5 The mountains shake before him, and the hills melt away. The earth trembles at his presence — the world and all who live in it.
6 Who can stand before his great anger? Who can endure his burning anger? His anger is poured out like fire. Even rocks are broken before him.
7 The LORD is good, a safe place in the day of trouble. He cares for those who trust in him.
8 But with a great flood he will destroy Nineveh. He will chase his enemies into darkness.
9 Whatever you plot against the LORD, he will bring it to an end. Trouble will not rise up a second time.
10 For they will be tangled like thorns and burned up like a drunkard's drink. They will be like dry stubble.
11 From you, O Nineveh, comes one who plots evil against the LORD, an adviser of wickedness.
12 This is what the LORD says: "Though they are many and joined together, they will be cut down and pass away. Though I have hurt you, O Judah, I will hurt you no longer.
13 For I will now break their yoke from your neck. I will tear away your chains."

14 The LORD has given a command about you, O Nineveh: "There will be no children to carry on your name. I will cut off the carved idol and cast idol from the house of your gods. I will prepare your grave, for you are worthless."

15 Look to the mountains — the feet of one who brings good news, who proclaims peace! Celebrate your feasts, O Judah. Keep your promises. For the wicked will never again march through you. They will be completely cut off.

title: Nahum 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nahum
paragraphs: 1 The destroyer comes against you, O Nineveh. Guard the fort! Watch the road! Brace yourselves! Gather all your strength!
2 For the LORD will restore the glory of Jacob like the glory of Israel. Destroyers had ruined them and broken the branches of their vine.
3 The shields of his mighty men are red. The brave soldiers are dressed in scarlet. The metal of the chariots flashes like fire on the day they are prepared. The cypress spears are held up.
4 The chariots rush through the streets. They race around the plazas. They look like torches, darting about like lightning.
5 He calls his leaders. They stumble as they advance. They race to the wall. The siege shield is put in place.
6 The river gates are thrown open, and the palace falls.
7 It is decided that the city be carried into exile. Her servant women cry like doves and beat their chests.
8 Nineveh has been like a pool of water all her days. But now it is draining away. "Stop! Stop!" they cry, but no one turns back.
9 "Take the silver! Take the gold!" There is no end to the treasure, plenty of every precious thing.
10 She is empty! Yes, she is empty and laid waste! Hearts melt, knees knock, bodies tremble. Every face grows pale.
11 Where is the lions' den, the feeding ground of the young lions? Where the lion and lioness walked with their cubs, with nothing to scare them away?
12 The lion killed enough for its cubs and choked prey for the lioness. It filled its dens with the kill, its lairs with killed prey.

13 "Look, I am against you," declares the LORD of Hosts. "I will burn your chariots in smoke. The sword will eat up your young lions. I will cut off your prey from the earth. The voices of your messengers will no longer be heard."

title: Nahum 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Nahum
paragraphs: 1 Woe to the bloody city, full of lies, full of stolen goods, never without prey.
2 The crack of the whip, the rumble of the wheel, galloping horse and bounding chariot!
3 Charging horseman, flashing sword, shining spear. Heaps of the slain, piles of dead bodies, corpses without end. They stumble over their dead —
4 because of the many unfaithfulnesses of the prostitute, the seductive mistress of magic. She betrays nations by her prostitution and clans by her witchcraft.
5 "Look, I am against you," declares the LORD of Hosts. "I will pull up your skirts over your face. I will show your nakedness to the nations and your shame to the kingdoms.
6 I will pelt you with mud and treat you with contempt. I will make a shameful sight of you.
7 Then all who see you will turn away and say, 'Nineveh is destroyed. Who will grieve for her?' Where can I find comforters for you?"
8 Are you better than Thebes, set by the Nile with water around her? Her wall was the sea, her wall was the water.
9 Cush and Egypt were her endless strength. Put and Libya were her allies.
10 Yet she became an exile. She went into captivity. Her babies were smashed to pieces at the head of every street. They cast lots for her important men. All her nobles were bound in chains.
11 You too will become drunk. You will go into hiding and seek shelter from the enemy.
12 All your strong forts are fig trees with the first ripe figs. When shaken, they fall into the mouth of the eater!
13 Look at your troops — they are like women! The gates of your land are wide open to your enemies. Fire eats up their bars.
14 Draw your water for the siege. Strengthen your strong forts. Work the clay and tread the mortar. Repair the brick oven!
15 There the fire will eat you up. The sword will cut you down and eat you like a young locust. Make yourself many like the young locust. Make yourself many like the swarming locust!
16 You have multiplied your merchants more than the stars of the sky. The young locust strips the land and flies away.
17 Your guards are like the swarming locust. Your scribes are like clouds of locusts that settle on the walls on a cold day. When the sun rises, they fly away, and no one knows where.
18 O king of Assyria, your shepherds are asleep. Your officers sleep. Your people are scattered on the mountains, with no one to gather them.
19 There is no healing for your injury. Your wound is deep. All who hear the news of you clap at your fall. For who has not suffered your constant cruelty?
